title: Canthium perakanthus
intro: Canthium perakanthus, also known as the Malay teatree, is a species of plant in the Rubiaceae family, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, with its health benefits attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Canthium perakanthus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Canthium perakanthus is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Canthium perakanthus is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the genus Canthium, which consists of various flowering plants native to tropical regions. The plant is closely related to other species within the Rubiaceae family, including gardenias and coffee. The classification of Canthium perakanthus is further divided into its kingdom, family, order, class, and other taxonomic ranks. The specific classification of Canthium perakanthus is as follows: kingdom Plantae, family Rubiaceae, order Gentianales, class Magnoliopsida.
